seoTitle: Central Mangrove vs Central Tilba — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Central Mangrove and Central Tilba. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Central Mangrove vs Central Tilba: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Central Mangrove and Central Tilba.
heading: Central Mangrove vs Central Tilba — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Central Mangrove, New South Wales and Central Tilba, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Central Tilba has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Central Mangrove (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Central Mangrove or Central Tilba safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Central Mangrove has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Central Tilba scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Central Mangrove scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Central Tilba scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Central Mangrove or Central Tilba?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Central Tilba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
